name: Portkey Admin MCP Server
description: MCP server for the Portkey Admin API. Manage prompts, configs, analytics, API keys, providers, guardrails, limits, logs, users, workspaces, and MCP integrations from any MCP client.
author: CodesWhat

prompts.name: plan_portkey_admin_workflow
prompts.title: Plan Portkey Admin Workflow
prompts.description: Create a concise, safe plan for a Portkey Admin API task using this MCP server.

arguments.name: task
arguments.description: Portkey admin task to plan, such as promoting a prompt

arguments.name: area
arguments.description: Optional Portkey area, such as prompts, analytics, configs, or users

resources.name: workflow-guide
resources.title: Portkey Admin Workflow Guide
resources.description: Operational guidance for using Portkey Admin MCP tools safely and effectively.

tools.name: list_all_users
tools.description: List accepted org users with id, name, email, role, and timestamps. Use this to find a user_id before get_user, update_user, delete_user, or add_workspace_member; use list_user_invites for pending invitations. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

tools.name: invite_user
tools.description: Invite a new org user and optionally provision workspace access and an API key in one call. Workspace assignments apply only after acceptance; use add_workspace_member or update_workspace_member later for follow-up changes.

email.description: Email address of the user to invite

role.description: Organization-level role: 'admin' for full access, 'member' for limited access

id.description: Workspace ID/slug where the user will be granted access

role.description: Workspace-level role: 'admin' for full access, 'manager' for workspace management, 'member' for basic access

workspaces.description: List of workspaces and corresponding roles to grant to the user

workspace_api_key_details.description: Optional API key to be created for the user

name.description: Name of the API key to be created

expiry.description: Expiration date for the API key (ISO8601 format)

metadata.description: Additional metadata key-value pairs for the API key

scopes.description: List of permission scopes for the API key

tools.name: get_user_stats
tools.description: Return per-user request and cost analytics for a required time range. This is usage-by-user, not population metrics; use get_users_analytics for active-user or cohort trends. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

time_of_generation_min.description: Start time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

time_of_generation_max.description: End time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-02-01T00:00:00Z')

total_units_min.description: Minimum number of total tokens to filter by

total_units_max.description: Maximum number of total tokens to filter by

cost_min.description: Minimum cost in cents to filter by

cost_max.description: Maximum cost in cents to filter by

status_code.description: Filter by specific HTTP status codes (comma-separated)

virtual_keys.description: Filter by specific virtual key slugs (comma-separated)

page_size.description: Number of results per page (for pagination)

tools.name: get_user
tools.description: Get one accepted user by id and return their profile, role, and timestamps. Use list_all_users to find the id if you only have a name or email, and get_user_invite for pending invitations. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

tools.name: update_user
tools.description: Update a user's first name, last name, or organization role by id. Email and workspace roles are not editable here; use update_workspace_member for workspace membership changes.

tools.name: delete_user
tools.description: Delete a user from the org by id. This is permanent, removes org and workspace memberships, revokes API keys, and ends active sessions; use delete_user_invite for pending invites instead.

tools.name: list_user_invites
tools.description: List pending and sent invitations with id, email, role, status, and expiry. Use this to check invite state; use list_all_users for users who already accepted. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

tools.name: get_user_invite
tools.description: Get one invitation by invite id and return its email, role, status, and expiry. Use this for pending invites only; use get_user for accepted users.

tools.name: delete_user_invite
tools.description: Delete a pending invite and revoke its invite link. This does not affect existing users; use delete_user for full user removal.

tools.name: resend_user_invite
tools.description: Resend the email for a pending invite that has not been accepted, unlike invite_user which creates a new invite. This sends a fresh email without modifying the invite record, expiry, or role; use get_user_invite first if you are unsure whether the invite still exists and list_user_invites to discover invite_ids.

tools.name: list_workspaces
tools.description: List workspaces with id, name, slug, default settings, and timestamps. Use this to find a workspace_id before get_workspace, update_workspace, add_workspace_member, or remove_workspace_member.

page_size.description: Number of workspaces to return per page (default varies by endpoint)

current_page.description: Page number to retrieve when results are paginated

tools.name: get_workspace
tools.description: Get one workspace by id and return its full details, including defaults and the complete member list. Use this when you need membership detail; use list_workspaces for an overview.

workspace_id.description: The unique identifier of the workspace to retrieve. This can be found in the workspace's URL or from the list_workspaces tool response

tools.name: create_workspace
tools.description: Create a workspace to isolate resources, API keys, and team members. If slug is omitted it is auto-generated from the name; returns the new workspace id, name, and slug.

slug.description: URL-friendly slug (auto-generated if not provided)

is_default.description: Set as default workspace (1 = yes, 0 = no)

metadata.description: Custom metadata key-value pairs

tools.name: update_workspace
tools.description: Update a workspace's name, slug, description, default flag, or metadata by id, unlike update_workspace_member which changes role assignments within a workspace. Only provided fields change and updates take effect immediately; changing the slug can break URLs, API key references, and other external links, so confirm no dependencies first.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID to update

slug.description: New slug for the workspace

is_default.description: Set as default workspace (1 = yes, 0 = no)

metadata.description: New metadata key-value pairs

tools.name: delete_workspace
tools.description: Delete a workspace by id. This is permanent and removes the workspace, its members, configs, API keys, and resources.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID to delete

tools.name: add_workspace_member
tools.description: Add an existing org user to a workspace with a role. Requires a UUID user_id; use list_all_users to find it, and invite_user first if the person is not yet in the org.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID to add the member to

user_id.description: The user ID to add (must be a valid UUID from list_all_users, not an email address)

tools.name: list_workspace_members
tools.description: List every member in a workspace with organization role, workspace role, status, and timestamps. Use this to find a user_id before get_workspace_member, update_workspace_member, or remove_workspace_member.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID to list members for

tools.name: get_workspace_member
tools.description: Get one workspace member by workspace_id and user_id. Use this when you already know both IDs; use list_workspace_members to browse the full roster.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID

tools.name: update_workspace_member
tools.description: Update a workspace member's role by workspace_id and user_id. Only the role changes here; use list_workspace_members or get_workspace_member to confirm the current assignment first.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID

tools.name: remove_workspace_member
tools.description: Remove a user from a workspace and revoke workspace access. This does not delete the user from the organization; use delete_user for full removal.

workspace_id.description: The workspace ID

tools.name: list_configs
tools.description: List configs in the org with id, slug, name, status, workspace, and timestamps. Use this summary view to find a slug; use get_config for the full routing, cache, retry, and target settings before updating or deleting.

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: Get one config by slug and return its routing, cache, retry, and target settings. Requires a known slug; use list_configs to discover one before editing.

slug.description: The unique identifier (slug) of the configuration to retrieve. This can be found in the configuration's URL or from the list_configs tool response

tools.name: create_config
tools.description: Create a config that defines routing, cache, retry, and targets for requests; use update_config to modify an existing one and list_config_versions for history. At least one setting is required, new configs become active immediately once referenced by a key or prompt, and the call returns the new id and version_id.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to create config in

retry_on_status_codes.description: HTTP status codes to retry on (e.g., [429, 500, 502, 503])

targets.description: Array of target providers with virtual keys

tools.name: update_config
tools.description: Update a config by slug and create a new version. Only provided fields change; name and status are editable, while the slug stays fixed. Use list_config_versions if you need history first.

slug.description: Configuration slug to update

retry_on_status_codes.description: HTTP status codes to retry on

tools.name: delete_config
tools.description: Delete a config by slug. This is permanent, removes all versions, and breaks anything still pointing at that slug; check list_config_versions first.

slug.description: Configuration slug to delete

tools.name: list_config_versions
tools.description: List every version of a config with version number, config payload, creator, and timestamp. Use this to audit history or compare revisions before update_config or delete_config.

slug.description: Configuration slug to list versions for

tools.name: list_virtual_keys
tools.description: List provider API keys stored as virtual keys in your Portkey org. Use this to find slugs before wiring prompts/configs or auditing limits. Returns total plus name, slug, status, usage limits, rate limits, reset state, and model config.

tools.name: create_virtual_key
tools.description: Store a provider API key as a virtual key. The raw key is encrypted and only returned at creation time, so save the returned slug and use it in prompts/configs. Optional usage and rate limits apply immediately, and the tool returns the new slug.

name.description: Display name for the virtual key

key.description: The actual provider API key to store

note.description: Optional note or description for this key

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to create the key in

alert_threshold.description: Percentage of credit_limit at which to send alert emails (0-100)

tools.name: get_virtual_key
tools.description: Fetch one virtual key by slug, including metadata, a masked secret, limits, status, and model config. Use this before updating or to inspect the current configuration.

slug.description: The unique slug identifier of the virtual key to retrieve

tools.name: update_virtual_key
tools.description: Update a virtual key's name, secret, note, or limits. Rotating the key takes effect immediately, and limit changes apply to downstream prompts and configs using this slug. Returns the updated name, slug, and status.

slug.description: The slug of the virtual key to update

name.description: New display name for the key

rate_limit_rpm.description: New rate limit in requests per minute

tools.name: delete_virtual_key
tools.description: Delete a virtual key by slug. This is irreversible and will break prompts and configs that reference the slug, so confirm no active dependencies first. Returns success after removal.

slug.description: The slug of the virtual key to delete

tools.name: create_api_key
tools.description: Create a Portkey API key for auth. Org keys grant broader access; workspace keys are scoped. WARNING: The key secret is returned ONCE in the tool result and will be visible in MCP transcripts and LLM context — store it securely immediately. Using the key grants access immediately according to its scopes, defaults, and limits. Workspace keys require workspace_id and user keys require user_id.

type.description: Key type: 'organisation' for org-wide access or 'workspace' for workspace-scoped

sub_type.description: Sub-type: 'user' for user-associated keys or 'service' for service accounts

name.description: Display name for the API key

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID (required for workspace-type keys)

user_id.description: User ID (required for user sub-type keys)

rate_limit_rpm.description: Rate limit in requests per minute

default_config_id.description: Default configuration ID to use with this key

tools.name: list_api_keys
tools.description: List Portkey API keys for auditing access, scopes, defaults, limits, and expiration. Use this for API keys only; use list_virtual_keys for provider keys. Returns total plus id, type, status, workspace/user scope, limits, defaults, alert emails, and creation mode.

workspace_id.description: Filter by workspace ID

tools.name: get_api_key
tools.description: Fetch one API key by UUID without revealing the secret. Use this to inspect scopes, defaults, limits, expiration, and reset state before changing access.

id.description: The UUID of the API key to retrieve

tools.name: update_api_key
tools.description: Update an API key's name, description, scopes, defaults, or limits, unlike delete_api_key which revokes it or create_api_key which issues a new one. Changes take effect immediately for downstream callers, type and sub-type stay fixed after creation, and the call returns success without rotating the secret.

id.description: The UUID of the API key to update

name.description: New display name for the key

scopes.description: New permission scopes for the key

rate_limit_rpm.description: New rate limit in requests per minute

expires_at.description: New expiration date in ISO 8601 format, or null to remove expiration

tools.name: delete_api_key
tools.description: Delete an API key by UUID. This cannot be undone, revokes access immediately, and can break active sessions using the key. Returns success after revocation.

id.description: The UUID of the API key to delete

tools.name: list_collections
tools.description: List prompt collections in the workspace, optionally filtering by name or workspace. Returns ids, names, slugs, and timestamps so you can choose a collection_id before create_prompt, get_collection, or list_prompts.

workspace_id.description: Filter by workspace ID

tools.name: create_collection
tools.description: Create a new prompt collection for organizing prompts by app. Use this when you need a new namespace before create_prompt; returns the collection id and slug, and does not move any prompts.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to create collection in

tools.name: get_collection
tools.description: Fetch one collection by id or slug and return its name, slug, workspace, and timestamps. Use list_collections when browsing and get_collection when you already know the target.

collection_id.description: Collection ID or slug to retrieve

tools.name: update_collection
tools.description: Update a collection's name or description only. This does not move prompts or change membership, so use it for metadata changes rather than reorganization.

tools.name: delete_collection
tools.description: Delete a prompt collection by ID. This cannot be undone; prompts stay in the workspace but lose their collection grouping, so reassign them first if organization matters.

tools.name: create_prompt
tools.description: Create a new prompt template and initial version. Use this for first-time setup; use migrate_prompt for idempotent CI/CD flows. Accepts plain text or structured chat messages, creates a new version immediately, and returns the prompt id, slug, and version id. For multi-message chat prompts pass messages (preferred) or a JSON-encoded array as string.

collection_id.description: Collection ID to organize the prompt in (use list_collections to find)

string.description: Legacy prompt template string. Use plain text for single-message prompts, or a JSON-encoded messages array string for multi-message chat prompts.

messages.description: Structured chat template alias. Serialized to the legacy string format before creation.

role.description: Message role in the chat template

items.description: Content block within a structured chat message
Structured chat message in a prompt template

virtual_key.description: Virtual key slug for model access

model.description: Model identifier (e.g., 'gpt-4', 'claude-3-opus'). Required unless ai_model_id or finetune_id is provided

ai_model_id.description: AI model ID (alternative to model). Required unless model or finetune_id is provided

finetune_id.description: Fine-tune ID (alternative to model). Required unless model or ai_model_id is provided

tools.description: Tool definitions for tool use

mode.description: Tool choice mode: auto lets the model decide, none disables tool use, function forces one function by name

function_name.description: Required when mode is 'function'; ignored otherwise

tools.name: list_prompts
tools.description: List prompts across the workspace, with optional collection, workspace, or search filters. Returns a paginated summary with id, name, slug, model, and status so you can choose a prompt_id before get_prompt, update_prompt, or render_prompt.

collection_id.description: Filter by collection ID (recommended for app-specific prompts)

workspace_id.description: Filter by workspace ID

tools.name: get_prompt
tools.description: Fetch a prompt's full definition, active version, and version history. Use this before updating, publishing, rendering, or copying a prompt when you need the stored template and metadata. For multi-message chat prompts pass messages (preferred) or a JSON-encoded array as string.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to retrieve

tools.name: update_prompt
tools.description: Update an existing prompt and create a new archived version. Only provided fields change, and publish_prompt is what makes the new version active. For multi-message chat prompts pass messages (preferred) or a JSON-encoded array as string.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to update

name.description: New display name for the prompt

string.description: Legacy prompt template string. Use plain text for single-message prompts, or a JSON-encoded messages array string for multi-message chat prompts.

messages.description: Structured chat template alias for updates. Serialized to the legacy string format before the prompt is updated.

role.description: Message role in the chat template

items.description: Content block within a structured chat message
Structured chat message in a prompt template

parameters.description: New default values for template variables

tools.description: New tool definitions

mode.description: Tool choice mode: auto lets the model decide, none disables tool use, function forces one function by name

function_name.description: Required when mode is 'function'; ignored otherwise

tools.name: delete_prompt
tools.description: Delete a prompt and all its versions by id. This cannot be undone, immediately breaks callers using the slug, and should only be used after checking list_prompt_versions or confirming you do not need an audit trail.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to delete

tools.name: publish_prompt
tools.description: Publish a specific version of a prompt as the active default, unlike promote_prompt which copies across environments or update_prompt which creates a new draft. This immediately routes all callers using the slug to that version and there is no rollback, so use list_prompt_versions to pick the version and update_prompt first if you need to create new content before promoting it.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to publish

version.description: Version number to publish as the default

tools.name: list_prompt_versions
tools.description: List all versions of one prompt, including version number, description, status, label, and a short template preview. Use this for history or to choose a version_id before publish_prompt or update_prompt_version.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to list versions for

tools.name: render_prompt
tools.description: Render a prompt by substituting variables and returning the final messages without calling the model. Use this to verify template output before a completion; run_prompt_completion is the tool that actually invokes the model.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to render

variables.description: Variable values to substitute into the template

tools.name: run_prompt_completion
tools.description: Execute a prompt against the configured model and return the completion. This makes a billable model call, so use render_prompt first when you want to check the template and validate_completion_metadata when billing fields are uncertain.

prompt_id.description: Prompt ID or slug to execute

variables.description: Variable values to substitute into the template

app.description: App identifier (REQUIRED). Use your deployed app name, for example 'hourlink' or 'support-console'.

env.description: Environment identifier (REQUIRED). Use your environment name, for example 'dev', 'staging', 'prod', or 'qa'.

metadata.description: Billing metadata - client_id, app, env are REQUIRED for cost attribution

tools.name: migrate_prompt
tools.description: Create or update a prompt in one idempotent step for CI/CD and prompt-as-code flows. Finds existing prompts by name within the collection, stores app/env in template_metadata, and supports dry_run for safe preflight checks.

name.description: Prompt name to create or find for update

app.description: App identifier (REQUIRED). Use your deployed app name, for example 'hourlink' or 'support-console'.

env.description: Environment identifier (REQUIRED). Use your environment name, for example 'dev', 'staging', 'prod', or 'qa'.

collection_id.description: Collection ID to search in and create under

string.description: Legacy prompt template string with {{variable}} mustache syntax.

messages.description: Structured chat template alias for migrations. Serialized to the legacy string format before the prompt is created or updated.

role.description: Message role in the chat template

items.description: Content block within a structured chat message
Structured chat message in a prompt template

virtual_key.description: Virtual key slug for model access

tools.description: Tool definitions

mode.description: Tool choice mode: auto lets the model decide, none disables tool use, function forces one function by name

function_name.description: Required when mode is 'function'; ignored otherwise

dry_run.description: When true, only check what action would be taken without making changes

tools.name: promote_prompt
tools.description: Copy a prompt from one environment to another and create or update the target automatically. Use this for staged releases when you want the target prompt synchronized without manual edits, and it returns both source and target version ids.

source_prompt_id.description: Source prompt ID or slug (e.g., staging prompt)

target_collection_id.description: Target collection ID for the promoted prompt

target_name.description: Target prompt name (defaults to source name with env suffix replaced)

target_env.description: Environment identifier (REQUIRED). Use your environment name, for example 'dev', 'staging', 'prod', or 'qa'.

virtual_key.description: Virtual key ID to use (defaults to source prompt's virtual_key)

tools.name: validate_completion_metadata
tools.description: Preflight billing metadata before run_prompt_completion. Validates required fields and values without making changes, so you can catch attribution errors before paying for the call.

app.description: App identifier (REQUIRED). Use your deployed app name, for example 'hourlink' or 'support-console'.

env.description: Environment identifier (REQUIRED). Use your environment name, for example 'dev', 'staging', 'prod', or 'qa'.

tools.name: get_prompt_version
tools.description: Retrieve a specific prompt version by its version UUID. Use list_prompt_versions to find the id first; returns the template, parameters, and model config for that version.

tools.name: update_prompt_version
tools.description: Update a specific prompt version's label assignment. This only assigns or removes a label, and null clears the label after you look up ids with list_prompt_labels.

label_id.description: Label ID to assign to this version, or null to remove the label

tools.name: get_cost_analytics
tools.description: Get cost time-series data with summary.total_cost, summary.average_cost_per_request, and per-bucket total/avg cost. Use this for spend analysis and spike detection; use get_token_analytics when you need token volume instead of monetary cost. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

time_of_generation_min.description: Start time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

time_of_generation_max.description: End time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-02-01T00:00:00Z')

total_units_min.description: Minimum number of total tokens to filter by

total_units_max.description: Maximum number of total tokens to filter by

cost_min.description: Minimum cost in cents to filter by

cost_max.description: Maximum cost in cents to filter by

status_code.description: Legacy Portkey query param for HTTP status codes. Comma-separated string; prefer status_codes for structured inputs.

virtual_keys.description: Legacy Portkey query param for virtual key slugs. Comma-separated string; prefer virtual_key_slugs for structured inputs.

configs.description: Legacy Portkey query param for config slugs. Comma-separated string; prefer config_slugs for structured inputs.

status_codes.description: Structured alias for status_code. Use an array of HTTP status codes; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

virtual_key_slugs.description: Structured alias for virtual_keys. Use an array of virtual key slugs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

config_slugs.description: Structured alias for configs. Use an array of config slugs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

api_key_ids.description: Legacy Portkey query param for API key UUIDs. Comma-separated string; request_analytics also accepts an array and normalizes it to this form.

metadata.description: Legacy Portkey query param for metadata filtering. Stringified JSON object, e.g. '{"env":"prod","app":"myapp"}'; prefer metadata_filter for structured inputs.

ai_org_model.description: Legacy Portkey query param for provider/model pairs. Format: 'provider__model' with double underscore, e.g. 'openai__gpt-4' or 'anthropic__claude-3-opus'. Comma-separated string; prefer provider_models for structured inputs.

provider_models.description: Structured alias for ai_org_model. Use provider__model strings in an array; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

trace_id.description: Legacy Portkey query param for trace IDs. Comma-separated string; prefer trace_ids for structured inputs.

trace_ids.description: Structured alias for trace_id. Use an array of trace IDs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

span_id.description: Legacy Portkey query param for span IDs. Comma-separated string; prefer span_ids for structured inputs.

span_ids.description: Structured alias for span_id. Use an array of span IDs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

metadata_filter.description: Structured alias for metadata. Use an object such as { env: 'prod' }; normalized to a JSON string before the request is sent.

tools.name: get_request_analytics
tools.description: Get request-volume time-series data with summary.total_requests, summary.successful_requests, summary.failed_requests, and per-bucket total/success/failed counts. Use this for traffic and reliability trends; use get_error_analytics when you only need error counts. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

time_of_generation_max.description: End time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-02-01T00:00:00Z')

configs.description: Legacy Portkey query param for config slugs. Comma-separated string; prefer config_slugs for structured inputs.

virtual_key_slugs.description: Structured alias for virtual_keys. Use an array of virtual key slugs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

config_slugs.description: Structured alias for configs. Use an array of config slugs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

api_key_ids.description: Legacy Portkey query param for API key UUIDs. Comma-separated string; request_analytics also accepts an array and normalizes it to this form.

metadata.description: Legacy Portkey query param for metadata filtering. Stringified JSON object, e.g. '{"env":"prod","app":"myapp"}'; prefer metadata_filter for structured inputs.

ai_org_model.description: Legacy Portkey query param for provider/model pairs. Format: 'provider__model' with double underscore, e.g. 'openai__gpt-4' or 'anthropic__claude-3-opus'. Comma-separated string; prefer provider_models for structured inputs.

provider_models.description: Structured alias for ai_org_model. Use provider__model strings in an array; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

trace_id.description: Legacy Portkey query param for trace IDs. Comma-separated string; prefer trace_ids for structured inputs.

trace_ids.description: Structured alias for trace_id. Use an array of trace IDs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

span_id.description: Legacy Portkey query param for span IDs. Comma-separated string; prefer span_ids for structured inputs.

span_ids.description: Structured alias for span_id. Use an array of span IDs; normalized to the legacy comma-separated Portkey query param.

metadata_filter.description: Structured alias for metadata. Use an object such as { env: 'prod' }; normalized to a JSON string before the request is sent.

tools.name: get_token_analytics
tools.description: Get token-usage time-series data with summary.total_tokens, summary.prompt_tokens, summary.completion_tokens, and per-bucket total/prompt/completion counts. Use this for consumption trends; use get_cost_analytics when you need spend instead of token volume. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

time_of_generation_min.description: Start time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

time_of_generation_max.description: End time for the analytics period (ISO8601 format, e.g., '2024-02-01T00:00:00Z')

total_units_min.description: Minimum number of total tokens to filter by

total_units_max.description: Maximum number of total tokens to filter by

cost_min.description: Minimum cost in cents to filter by

tools.name: get_guardrail
tools.description: Fetch one guardrail by id or slug with its full checks and actions; use list_guardrails to discover ids first. Use before update_guardrail or delete_guardrail when you need the exact enforcement policy, and returns the full check and action configuration alongside status and ownership.

guardrail_id.description: The guardrail UUID or slug (with guard_ prefix) to retrieve

tools.name: create_guardrail
tools.description: Create a guardrail with checks and actions for request filtering. Create it first, then reference it from configs; the new version becomes the policy anchor for downstream use.

id.description: Check identifier (e.g., 'default.jwt', 'default.pii')

checks.description: Array of checks to apply (at least one required)

deny.description: Whether to deny the request on check failure

metadata.description: Additional metadata
Additional metadata

message.description: Message to return when guardrail triggers

actions.description: Actions to take when guardrail checks pass or fail

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to create the guardrail in

organisation_id.description: Organisation ID (required if workspace_id not provided)

tools.name: update_guardrail
tools.description: Update a guardrail's name, checks, or actions, unlike create_guardrail which registers a new one or delete_guardrail which removes it. This creates a new version that takes effect immediately for dependent configs, so review list_guardrails first; returns the updated id, slug, and version_id.

guardrail_id.description: The guardrail UUID or slug to update

checks.description: Updated array of checks to apply

id.description: Check identifier (e.g., 'default.jwt', 'default.pii')

deny.description: Whether to deny the request on check failure

metadata.description: Additional metadata
Additional metadata

message.description: Message to return when guardrail triggers

tools.name: delete_guardrail
tools.description: Delete a guardrail by id or slug. This is irreversible and removes the check from any configs that reference it, so review dependent configs first.

tools.name: list_rate_limits
tools.description: List rate limits in the org with id, type, unit, value, status, scope, conditions, and grouping. Use this to find an existing policy before get_rate_limit, update_rate_limit, or delete_rate_limit.

workspace_id.description: Filter rate limits by workspace ID

tools.name: get_rate_limit
tools.description: Get one rate limit by id and return its full conditions and grouping definition. Use list_rate_limits to discover ids or compare policies first.

id.description: The unique identifier of the rate limit

tools.name: create_rate_limit
tools.description: Create a request or token throttle with conditions, group_by, type, unit, and value. conditions and group_by are required; use usage limits when you need a cumulative budget instead.

conditions.description: Array of conditions that determine which requests this rate limit applies to

group_by.description: Array of fields to group the rate limit by (e.g., ['virtual_key'], ['api_key', 'user_id'])

unit.description: Time unit: 'rpm' (per minute), 'rph' (per hour), or 'rpd' (per day)

value.description: The maximum allowed value per unit (e.g., 100 rpm)

name.description: Optional name for the rate limit

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to scope the limit to

organisation_id.description: Organisation ID to scope the limit to

tools.name: update_rate_limit
tools.description: Update a rate limit's name, unit, or value by id. Conditions and group_by are immutable after creation; use get_rate_limit first if you need the full policy.

id.description: The unique identifier of the rate limit

name.description: New name for the rate limit

unit.description: New time unit: 'rpm' (per minute), 'rph' (per hour), or 'rpd' (per day)

value.description: New maximum allowed value per unit

tools.name: delete_rate_limit
tools.description: Delete a rate limit by id. This is permanent and removes throttling immediately; review dependent configs and virtual keys before deleting.

id.description: The unique identifier of the rate limit

tools.name: list_usage_limits
tools.description: List usage limits in the org with id, type, credit_limit, status, reset schedule, scope, conditions, and grouping. Use this before get_usage_limit, update_usage_limit, or delete_usage_limit.

workspace_id.description: Filter usage limits by workspace ID

tools.name: get_usage_limit
tools.description: Get one usage limit by id and return its full budget, threshold, grouping, and reset details. Use list_usage_limits to discover ids or compare policies first.

id.description: The unique identifier of the usage limit

tools.name: create_usage_limit
tools.description: Create a cumulative budget for cost or tokens with optional alerts and weekly or monthly resets. conditions and group_by are required; use rate limits when you need request throttling.

conditions.description: Array of conditions that determine which requests this usage limit applies to

group_by.description: Array of fields to group the usage limit by (e.g., ['virtual_key'], ['api_key', 'user_id'])

credit_limit.description: The maximum allowed usage (cost in dollars or token count)

name.description: Optional name for the usage limit

alert_threshold.description: Percentage threshold (0-100) at which to send an alert

periodic_reset.description: Automatically reset usage counters on this schedule

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to scope the limit to

organisation_id.description: Organisation ID to scope the limit to

tools.name: update_usage_limit
tools.description: Update a usage limit's name, credit_limit, alert_threshold, reset schedule, or reset target by id, unlike update_rate_limit which tunes request throttling. New values apply immediately to tracked usage, conditions and group_by are immutable after creation, and the call returns the updated id without clearing accumulated usage (use reset_usage_limit_entity for that).

id.description: The unique identifier of the usage limit

name.description: New name for the usage limit

reset_usage_for_value.description: Reset usage counters for a specific group_by value

tools.name: delete_usage_limit
tools.description: Delete a usage limit by id. This is permanent, removes the budget immediately, and clears tracked usage state; check list_usage_limit_entities first if you need impact.

id.description: The unique identifier of the usage limit

tools.name: list_usage_limit_entities
tools.description: List the entities currently tracked against a usage limit, including current usage. Use this to see who is near or over budget before reset_usage_limit_entity or delete_usage_limit.

tools.name: reset_usage_limit_entity
tools.description: Reset tracked usage for one entity under a usage limit. This changes accumulated usage for that entity only; use list_usage_limit_entities to confirm the target first.

entity_id.description: Entity ID to reset usage for

tools.name: list_audit_logs
tools.description: List audit log events for a Portkey workspace or organization. Returns paginated action-level records with actor, resource, metadata, and timestamps for compliance or incident review; use this instead of analytics when you need individual events, not aggregates. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

workspace_id.description: Filter audit logs by workspace ID

actor_id.description: Filter by the user ID who performed the action

start_time.description: Start of time range filter (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

end_time.description: End of time range filter (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-31T23:59:59Z')

tools.name: create_prompt_label
tools.description: Create a prompt label for tagging prompt versions such as production, staging, or experiment. Requires either organisation_id or workspace_id to set scope, returns the new label id, and does not assign it to any versions yet.

organisation_id.description: Organisation ID to create the label in

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to create the label in

color_code.description: Hex format, e.g. '#FF5733'. Optional — omit for default color.

tools.name: list_prompt_labels
tools.description: List labels across the workspace or organisation, with optional search and scope filters. Returns ids, names, colors, status, and timestamps so you can choose a label_id before get_prompt_label or update_prompt_version.

workspace_id.description: Filter by workspace ID

tools.name: get_prompt_label
tools.description: Fetch one label's full definition, including scope, color, and status. Use this when you already know the label_id; list_prompt_labels is better for browsing candidates.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID for filtering

tools.name: update_prompt_label
tools.description: Update a prompt label's name, description, or color only, unlike update_prompt_version which changes which label a version carries. This takes effect immediately for all versions already tagged with the label, but does not reassign labels or touch history; use list_prompt_labels to find the label_id first.

tools.name: delete_prompt_label
tools.description: Delete a prompt label by ID. This cannot be undone; versions carrying the label lose it, and any workflow resolving by that label will need a replacement.

tools.name: create_prompt_partial
tools.description: Create a reusable prompt partial for inclusion with {{> partial_name}}. Use this for shared snippets or macros; returns the partial id, slug, and version id, and the new version stays inactive until published.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to create partial in (required for org-level API keys)

tools.name: list_prompt_partials
tools.description: List partials across collections, with optional collection filtering. Returns ids, slugs, names, collections, and status so you can choose a prompt_partial_id before get_prompt_partial, update_prompt_partial, delete_prompt_partial, or publish_partial.

collection_id.description: Filter by collection ID. Optional — omit to list all partials across collections

tools.name: get_prompt_partial
tools.description: Fetch a partial's content and current version details. Use this before embedding, updating, or checking what {{> partial_name}} resolves to; returns the stored string plus version metadata.

prompt_partial_id.description: Prompt partial ID or slug to retrieve

tools.name: update_prompt_partial
tools.description: Create a new version of a partial by updating its content or metadata. Only provided fields change, and the new version stays inactive until publish_partial makes it current.

prompt_partial_id.description: Prompt partial ID or slug to update

name.description: New display name for the partial

tools.name: delete_prompt_partial
tools.description: Delete a prompt partial by ID. This cannot be undone, and prompts that reference it with {{> name}} will fail to render until you replace the reference.

prompt_partial_id.description: Prompt partial ID or slug to delete

tools.name: list_partial_versions
tools.description: List all versions for one partial, including version numbers, descriptions, status, and timestamps. Use this when you need history or want to choose a version_id before publish_partial.

prompt_partial_id.description: Prompt partial ID or slug to list versions for

tools.name: publish_partial
tools.description: Publish a specific partial version as the default, unlike update_prompt_partial which creates a new draft without activating it. Use after list_partial_versions to pick a version_id; this immediately changes what {{> partial_name}} resolves to for all prompts and replaces the previously active version without a rollback path.

version.description: Version number to publish as default

tools.name: create_feedback
tools.description: Create feedback for a trace or request. Writes a new feedback record linked by trace_id, returns the created feedback IDs and status, and takes effect immediately; use update_feedback when correcting an existing record.

trace_id.description: The trace ID to associate the feedback with. This links feedback to a specific request/generation.

value.description: Feedback value/rating. Common patterns: 1 for positive (thumbs up), 0 for negative (thumbs down), or use a scale like 1-5.

weight.description: Optional weighting factor for the feedback. Use to give more importance to certain feedback.

metadata.description: Optional custom metadata for categorization and analysis (e.g., feedback_source, category, user_segment).

tools.name: update_feedback
tools.description: Update an existing feedback record by ID. Returns the updated status and feedback IDs, changes only value, weight, and metadata, and leaves the trace linkage immutable; use create_feedback only for a new record.

id.description: The unique identifier of the feedback to update

value.description: New feedback value/rating. Common patterns: 1 for positive, 0 for negative.

weight.description: New weighting factor for the feedback

metadata.description: New or updated custom metadata for the feedback

tools.name: insert_log
tools.description: Insert log records for requests that bypassed the gateway. This writes request, response, and trace metadata into Portkey immediately, and the call will fail if request_provider does not match a configured integration. Use the span fields to stitch trace hierarchies together.

tools.name: create_log_export
tools.description: Create a log export definition with filters and requested fields. This only sets up the export and does not start processing; call start_log_export next, then use get_log_export or download_log_export to inspect or retrieve the finished result.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID for the export

description.description: Human-readable description for the export job

time_min.description: Minimum time filter in date format (e.g., '2024-01-01' or ISO 8601)

time_max.description: Maximum time filter in date format (e.g., '2024-01-31' or ISO 8601)

cost_min.description: Minimum cost filter

cost_max.description: Maximum cost filter

total_units_min.description: Minimum total units (tokens) filter

total_units_max.description: Maximum total units (tokens) filter

ai_model.description: Filter by specific AI model names

requested_fields.description: Fields to include in export: id, trace_id, created_at, request, response, is_success, ai_org, ai_model, req_units, res_units, total_units, request_url, cost, cost_currency, response_time, response_status_code, mode, config, prompt_slug, metadata

tools.name: list_log_exports
tools.description: List log export jobs in a workspace with status, filters, and timestamps. Use this to find an export_id before calling get_log_export, start_log_export, cancel_log_export, or download_log_export.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID to list exports for (required)

tools.name: get_log_export
tools.description: Fetch one log export job by export_id and return its status, filters, requested fields, and file metadata. Use this when you already know the target; use list_log_exports for a workspace-wide overview.

export_id.description: The unique ID of the log export

tools.name: start_log_export
tools.description: Start processing a previously created log export job. This is asynchronous, only queues the export, and does not return rows or a download file; use get_log_export to poll progress and download_log_export after the job completes.

export_id.description: The unique ID of the log export to start

tools.name: cancel_log_export
tools.description: Cancel a pending or running log export job, unlike start_log_export which queues one or delete_integration which removes the source. This permanently stops that export, takes effect immediately, and does not roll back already-processed rows; call create_log_export and start_log_export again to retry.

export_id.description: The unique ID of the log export to cancel

tools.name: download_log_export
tools.description: Get a signed URL for downloading a completed log export. The export must already be finished; use get_log_export to confirm readiness and start_log_export if it has not run yet.

export_id.description: The unique ID of the log export to download

tools.name: update_log_export
tools.description: Update an existing log export configuration before or between export runs. Only workspace_id, time_of_generation_max, and requested_fields can change after creation, so use get_log_export to review the current job and start_log_export after the definition is ready.

export_id.description: The unique ID of the log export to update

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID for the export

time_of_generation_max.description: Maximum time filter in date format (e.g., '2024-07-25' or ISO 8601)

requested_fields.description: Fields to include in export: id, trace_id, created_at, request, response, is_success, ai_org, ai_model, req_units, res_units, total_units, request_url, cost, cost_currency, response_time, response_status_code, mode, config, prompt_slug, metadata

tools.name: list_providers
tools.description: List workspace-scoped provider instances and their limits or status. Use this to find provider slugs for workspace-level updates; use list_integrations for the org-level source connection. Returns total plus provider name, slug, integration, status, limits, expiration, and reset flags.

page_size.description: Number of results per page (max 100, default 50)

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID - required when using organization admin keys, optional with workspace API keys

tools.name: create_provider
tools.description: Create a workspace provider backed by an org integration. The provider inherits the integration key, but its limits and expiration are enforced independently for that workspace. Returns the new provider id and slug.

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID - required when using organization admin API keys

slug.description: Custom slug for the provider. Auto-generated with random suffix if omitted

note.description: Optional note or description for the provider

usage_limit_type.description: Type of usage limit: 'cost' (monetary) or 'tokens' (token count). Defaults to 'cost'.

periodic_reset.description: Period for resetting usage limits: 'monthly' or 'weekly'. Defaults to 'monthly'.

rate_limit_value.description: Must be provided together with rate_limit_unit.

rate_limit_unit.description: Must be provided together with rate_limit_value. Values: 'rpm' (requests/min), 'rph' (requests/hour), or 'rpd' (requests/day).

tools.name: get_provider
tools.description: Fetch one provider by slug, including limits, rate settings, expiration, and reset status. Use this to check consumption or audit configuration before updating.

slug.description: The unique slug identifier of the provider to retrieve

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID - required when using organization admin keys

tools.name: update_provider
tools.description: Update a provider's metadata, limits, or expiration. reset_usage clears accumulated usage counters immediately, so use it only when you intend to reset quota tracking. Returns the updated provider id and slug.

slug.description: The slug of the provider to update

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID - required when using organization admin keys

name.description: New display name for the provider

note.description: New note or description for the provider

usage_limit_type.description: Type of usage limit: 'cost' (monetary) or 'tokens' (token count). Defaults to 'cost'.

periodic_reset.description: Period for resetting usage limits: 'monthly' or 'weekly'. Defaults to 'monthly'.

rate_limit_unit.description: Rate limit unit: 'rpm' (requests per minute), 'rph' (requests per hour), or 'rpd' (requests per day)

expires_at.description: New expiration date in ISO 8601 format

reset_usage.description: Set to true to reset accumulated usage metrics

tools.name: delete_provider
tools.description: Delete a workspace provider by slug. This is irreversible and will break prompts, configs, and virtual keys that reference it; use delete_integration for the org source instead. Returns success after the provider is removed.

slug.description: The slug of the provider to delete

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID - required when using organization admin keys

tools.name: list_integrations
tools.description: List org-level AI provider connections with optional workspace or type filters. Use this to find integration slugs before model or workspace updates. Returns total plus id, name, slug, provider, status, description, workspace counts, and config summary.

page_size.description: Number of results per page (default 100, max 100)

workspace_id.description: Filter integrations accessible by a specific workspace

tools.name: create_integration
tools.description: Create an org-level provider integration. Some backends need provider-specific fields, and the new integration becomes the source for downstream providers and workspace access. Returns the new integration id and slug.

slug.description: URL-friendly identifier (auto-generated from name if not provided)

key.description: API key for the provider (if required)

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID for workspace-scoped integrations

aws_access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID (for AWS Bedrock)

aws_secret_access_key.description: AWS secret access key (for AWS Bedrock)

custom_host.description: Custom base URL for the provider

tools.name: get_integration
tools.description: Fetch one integration by slug, including masked key, workspace access, allowed models, and configuration metadata. Use this before editing provider-specific settings or auditing access. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

slug.description: The unique slug identifier of the integration to retrieve

tools.name: update_integration
tools.description: Update an integration's name, key, or provider-specific config. Key and config changes take effect immediately and can disrupt dependent providers or live requests.

slug.description: The slug of the integration to update

name.description: New human-readable name for the integration

key.description: New API key for the provider

aws_access_key_id.description: New AWS access key ID (for AWS Bedrock)

aws_secret_access_key.description: New AWS secret access key (for AWS Bedrock)

vertex_project_id.description: New GCP project ID (for Vertex AI)

custom_host.description: New custom base URL for the provider

tools.name: delete_integration
tools.description: Delete an integration by slug. This is irreversible and stops the org-level connection, which will break dependent virtual keys, providers, and workspace access.

slug.description: The slug of the integration to delete

tools.name: list_integration_models
tools.description: List models enabled on an integration. Use this to verify model availability before creating prompts or configs. Returns total plus model ids, display names, enabled state, and custom-model markers. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

slug.description: The slug of the integration

tools.name: update_integration_models
tools.description: Enable, disable, or register custom models for an integration. This changes model availability for every workspace using it, so confirm the downstream impact first. Returns success and the number of models updated.

slug.description: The slug of the integration
The model slug identifier

model_name.description: Display name for the model (required for custom models)

is_custom.description: Whether this is a custom model (default: false)

models.description: Array of model configurations to update

tools.name: delete_integration_model
tools.description: Delete a custom model from an integration. Built-in models should be disabled instead, because deletion only applies to custom entries. Returns success after the custom model is removed.

slug.description: The slug of the integration

model_slug.description: The slug of the model to delete

tools.name: list_integration_workspaces
tools.description: List workspaces that can use an integration, with their limits. Use this to audit access or confirm per-workspace cost and rate settings. Returns total plus workspace ids, names, enabled state, usage limits, and rate limits. Enterprise-gated. Returns 403 on non-Enterprise Portkey plans.

slug.description: The slug of the integration

tools.name: update_integration_workspaces
tools.description: Control which workspaces can use an integration and set per-workspace limits, unlike update_integration which edits the org-level connection. Call list_integration_workspaces first to review current state; access changes and new limits apply to downstream usage immediately, and the call returns success plus the number of workspaces updated.

slug.description: The slug of the integration

id.description: The workspace ID

enabled.description: Whether the workspace has access to this integration

rate_limit_rpm.description: Rate limit in requests per minute

workspaces.description: Array of workspace configurations to update

tools.name: list_mcp_integrations
tools.description: List MCP integrations in the organization. Returns paginated integration records plus total and has_more for discovering integration IDs; use get_mcp_integration for one integration's full Portkey-side config and list_mcp_servers for the servers under an integration.

workspace_id.description: Filter by workspace ID

tools.name: create_mcp_integration
tools.description: Create an MCP integration from an external server URL. Registers the Portkey-side connection and returns the new id and slug; if auth_type is headers, custom_headers are required, and you usually follow with create_mcp_server and capability updates.

name.description: Display name for the MCP integration

url.description: URL endpoint of the MCP server to integrate

transport.description: MCP transport protocol: 'http' (streamable HTTP) or 'sse' (server-sent events)

slug.description: Custom slug. Auto-generated if omitted

custom_headers.description: Custom headers for authentication (e.g. { "Authorization": "Bearer xxx" }). Sent via configurations.custom_headers

workspace_id.description: Workspace ID — required when using organization admin API keys

tools.name: get_mcp_integration
tools.description: Retrieve one MCP integration by id or slug. Returns the full Portkey-side config, including auth type, transport, and masked configuration keys; use get_mcp_integration_metadata for the server's self-reported metadata.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug to retrieve

tools.name: update_mcp_integration
tools.description: Update an MCP integration's name, description, URL, auth, or transport. Changes apply immediately and altering url or auth_type can break connected clients; use update_mcp_server when you only need to rename or re-describe a server.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug to update

custom_headers.description: New custom headers for authentication. Sent via configurations.custom_headers

tools.name: delete_mcp_integration
tools.description: Delete an MCP integration and all servers beneath it. This is irreversible, removes connected access immediately, and should only be used after confirming nothing depends on the integration.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug to delete

tools.name: get_mcp_integration_metadata
tools.description: Retrieve the external MCP server's self-reported metadata for an integration. Returns name, version, protocol, capability flags, and sync status; use get_mcp_integration for the Portkey-side connection config.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug

tools.name: list_mcp_integration_capabilities
tools.description: List capabilities exposed by the external MCP server for an integration. Returns total plus enabled-state entries so you can decide what to toggle; use before update_mcp_integration_capabilities when you need to compare the current surface.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug

tools.name: update_mcp_integration_capabilities
tools.description: Bulk enable or disable capabilities on an MCP integration. Changes take effect immediately for connected users and hide or expose the selected tools, resources, and prompts; use list_mcp_integration_capabilities first if you need the current state.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug

tools.name: list_mcp_integration_workspaces
tools.description: List which workspaces can access an MCP integration. Returns the global access mode plus per-workspace enablement for audit or permission review; use before update_mcp_integration_workspaces.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug

tools.name: update_mcp_integration_workspaces
tools.description: Grant or revoke workspace access to an MCP integration in bulk. Changes take effect immediately for all users in the selected workspaces; use list_mcp_integration_workspaces first to review the current access state.

id.description: The MCP integration ID or slug
Workspace ID

tools.name: list_mcp_servers
tools.description: List MCP servers in the organization. Returns paginated server records plus total for discovering server IDs; use get_mcp_server for one server's details and list_mcp_integrations for the parent integration.

workspace_id.description: Filter by workspace ID

tools.name: create_mcp_server
tools.description: Create an MCP server under an existing integration. Registers the server and returns the new id and slug; use list_mcp_integrations first to find the parent integration, then capabilities or access tools to configure it.

name.description: Display name for the MCP server

mcp_integration_id.description: ID or slug of the MCP integration this server belongs to

slug.description: Custom slug. Auto-generated if omitted

tools.name: get_mcp_server
tools.description: Retrieve one MCP server by id or slug. Returns server details including the parent integration, status, and created time; use get_mcp_server when you need the server record rather than the integration config.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug to retrieve

tools.name: update_mcp_server
tools.description: Update an MCP server's name or description. Changes apply immediately, but URL and auth live on the parent integration, so use update_mcp_integration for those fields.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug to update

tools.name: delete_mcp_server
tools.description: Delete an MCP server instance. This is irreversible, removes connected users' access immediately, and should be used only after confirming no workflows depend on the server.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug to delete

tools.name: test_mcp_server
tools.description: Test connectivity to an MCP server. Sends a live check and returns success, response time, HTTP status, and any error; use this before changing configuration or when diagnosing reachability.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug to test

tools.name: list_mcp_server_capabilities
tools.description: List capabilities exposed by an MCP server instance. Returns total plus the current tool, resource, and prompt surface; use this instead of the integration-level capability list when you need server-specific exposure.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug

tools.name: update_mcp_server_capabilities
tools.description: Enable or disable capabilities on an MCP server. Changes take effect immediately and override the integration-level settings for this server; use list_mcp_server_capabilities first to inspect the current surface.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug

tools.name: list_mcp_server_user_access
tools.description: List per-user access for an MCP server. Returns the default access mode, override flags, and connection status so you can audit who can use it; use before update_mcp_server_user_access.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug

tools.name: update_mcp_server_user_access
tools.description: Grant or revoke individual user access to an MCP server. Changes take effect immediately and override the default access setting for the selected users; use list_mcp_server_user_access first if you need the current state.

id.description: The MCP server ID or slug
